title: larbrea uliginosa
intro: Larbrea uliginosa, also known as marsh tea or marsh cudweed, is a perennial herb native to wetland areas in North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oil and flavonoid compounds. The health benefits of Larbrea uliginosa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Larbrea uliginosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
